docs.text: see the NixOS Wiki for helpful notes.

docs.title: Building a configuration.nix the nixos-unstable channel
docs.text:
{
  inputs = {
    nixpkgs.url = "nixpkgs/nixos-unstable";
  };
  outputs = { self, nixpkgs }: {
    # replace 'joes-desktop' with your hostname here.
    nixosConfigurations.media = nixpkgs.lib.nixosSystem {
      system = "x86_64-linux";
      modules = [ ./configuration.nix ];
    };
  };
}

docs.title: ZFS on NixOS
docs.text: see the NixOS Wiki on ZFS, or look up specific options using the NixOS options search

docs.title: Example Configuration Settings
docs.text:
# Networking
networking.hostId = "4f98920a"; # used to make sure the pool is imported correctly

# Basic ZFS Settings
boot.supportedFilesystems = [ "zfs" ]; # enable zfs
boot.zfs.forceImportRoot = false; # force the import of zfs root pools
boot.zfs.extraPools = [ "my-pool" "my-other-pool" ]; # mount the pools listed on boot

# Autoscrubbing settings
services.zfs.autoScrub = {
  enable = true; # enable autoscrubbing
  interval = monthly; # default interval for autoscrub is every month
};

# Autosnapshot settings
services.zfs.autoSnapshot = {
  enable = true; # enable snapshots
  montly = 12; # number of monthly snapshots to keep
  weekly = 52; # number of weekly snapshots to keep
  daily = 7; # dido
  hourly = 24; # dido
  frequent = 4; # number of 15 min interval snapshots to keep
};

docs.title: OpenWRT/Tailscale on Raspberry Pi 4
docs.text:
OpenWRT version 23.05 Raspberry Pi 4

see this OpenWRT wiki page for the basic setup guide see this OpenWRT wiki page for all things Raspberry Pi

docs.title: OpenWRT/Tailscale on Raspberry Pi 4
docs.text:
opkg update
opkg install tailscale
opkg install iptables-nft kmod-ipt-conntrack kmod-ipt-conntrack-extra kmod-ipt-conntrack-label kmod-nft-nat 
opkg kmod-ipt-nat

docs.title: Enable Case Fan
docs.text:
see here

opkg update
opkg install kmod-hwmon-gpiofan
/boot/config.txt
# temperature in milliCelsius
dtoverlay=gpio-fan,gpiopin=14,temp=80000

docs.title: LAN network configuration
docs.text:
/etc/config/network
config interface 'lan'
        option device 'br-lan'
        option proto 'static'
        option ipaddr '192.168.10.1'
        option netmask '255.255.255.0'
        option ip6assign '60'
        list dns '1.1.1.1'
        list dns '8.8.8.8'

docs.title: Tailscale interface
docs.text:
config interface tailscale
    option device 'tailscale0'
    option proto 'none'

docs.title: Configure Wireless WAN (for Raspberry Pi 4)
docs.text:
``` title="/etc/config/wireless config wifi-device 'radio0' option type 'mac80211' option path 'platform/soc/fe300000.mmcnr/mmc_host/mmc1/mmc1:0001/mmc1:0001:1' option channel '7' option band '5g' option htmode 'HT20' option hwmode '11g' option short_gi_40 '0' option disabled '0'

config wifi-iface 'wifinet1' option device 'radio0' option mode 'sta' option network 'wwan' option ssid 'Lothlorien' option encryption 'psk2' option key 'EnChus81222' 

## Configure firewall rules for tailscale
#### Add a new zone
``` title="/etc/config/firewall"
config zone
    option name 'tailscale
    list network 'tailscale'
    option input 'ACCEPT'
    option output 'ACCEPT'
    option forward 'ACCEPT'
    option masq '1'
    option mtu_fix '1'

config forwarding 
    option src 'tailscale'
    option dest 'lan'

# At the end of the file
config rule
    option name 'Allow Tailscale UDP'
    option src '*'
    option target 'ACCEPT'
    option proto 'udp'
    option dest_port '41641'

config forwarding
    option src 'lan'
    option dest 'tailscale'

docs.title: ZFS Commands
docs.text: See Oracle's Documentation Guide for more detailed information and examples.

docs.title: Create a mirror pool
docs.text:
Create a new pool called new-pool using the devices /dev/sdb and /dev/sdc in a mirror 

sudo zpool create new-pool mirror /dev/sdb /dev/sdc
You can also use the -m option to specify the mount point. The default mount point is /poolname 
sudo zpool create -m /usr/sharepool new-pool mirror /dev/sdb /dev/sdc

docs.title: Checking the status of all zfs pools
docs.text: sudo zpool status

docs.title: Add devices to an existing ZFS pool
docs.text:
Add the devices /dev/sdb and /dev/sdc to the pool called existing-pool 

zpool add existing-pool mirror /dev/sdb /dev/sdc

docs.title: Create a new dataset
docs.text:
Create a dataset named 'mydataset' on the pool 'mypool' 

sudo zfs create mypool/mydataset

docs.title: Check the dataset seize
docs.text: sudo zfs list mypool/dataset

docs.title: Set additional properties
docs.text:
sudo zfs set compression=on mypool/mydataset # turns on compression
sudo zfs set quota=10G mypool/mydataset # set the sixe quota to 10GB
sudo zfs get quota /mypool/mydataset # view the quota of the dataset
sudo zfs set canmount=on and mountpoint=/mount/point mypool/mydataset # these two options mount the dataset at boot

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text: zfs allow

docs.title: Basics
docs.text: ZFS is: - a COW (copy on write) filesystem AND a LVM (logical volume manager)

docs.title: Terms
docs.text:
• VDEV: virutal device. a logical grouping of one or more physical devices
  • Pool: a group or one or more VDEVS
  • RAId-Z: a way of putting multiple drives together that a
